title: Cluster Networking, CNI, NetworkPolicy, Dual Stack, and Layered Troubleshooting · Cluster Repair Bay
summary: An incident responder inherits a plausible but defective network intent and isolation evidence policy; preserve status and events, isolate the first causal fault in cluster networking, cni, networkpolicy, dual stack, and layered troubleshooting, and retain a regression check. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in network intent and isolation evidence policy and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, chang…
objectives: Trace Pod addressing, routing, CNI responsibilities, Service implementation, node paths, NAT, MTU, and platform-specific exceptions.
Interpret single or dual-stack families, family policy, ClusterIPs, EndpointSlices, service proxies, source addresses, and asymmetric reachability.
Derive ingress and egress isolation from Pod and namespace selectors, ipBlock, ports, peers, policy types, and additive allow rules.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# kubernetes-basics cumulative orchestration portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never invoke kubectl, read kubeconfig, contact a cluster API, or execute host commands.
workspace: kubernetes
version-baseline:
  kubernetes: 1.36.2
  minikube: 1.38.1
  gateway-api: 1.5.1
  helm: 4.2.2
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-cluster
evidence:

steps.title: Inspect the failure record · Debug K8s Cni Cluster Network Model
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug Pod can resolve DNS but cannot reach a backend after default deny; the policy selects only ingress, misses egress DNS, and assumes NetworkPolicy implementation behavior. Trace source, destination, port, direction, selector, CNI, and node boundary. Competency probe: Trace Pod addressing, routing, CNI responsibilities, Service implementation, node paths, NAT, MTU, and platform-specific exceptions.

Inspect the K8s Cni Cluster Network Model trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: Inspect the failure record · Debug K8s Cni Cluster Network Model.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for trace pod addressing, routing, cni responsibilities, service implementation, node paths, nat, mtu, and platform-specific exceptions. For Inspect the failure record · Debug K8s Cni Cluster Network Model, keep K8s Cni Cluster Network Model connected to mechanically reviewable Kubernetes object, status, and transfer evidence.

Trace source, destination, port, direction, selector, CNI, and node boundary. steps.title: Implement the cause-level repair · Debug K8s Network Layered Diagnosis
steps.instruction:
Append the Kubernetes evidence item headed "  # Evidence: k8s-network-layered-diagnosis-12a5kw". Tailor its network-policy.yaml change to the current stakeholder case, retain a deterministic API or manifest review command, reject one concrete failure, and record a repair. The browser analyzes text only; run kubectl, Helm, kubeadm, and cluster commands later in an authorized disposable transfer environment.

Evidence target: Implement the cause-level repair · Debug K8s Network Layered Diagnosis.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns K8s Network Layered Diagnosis into observable performance evidence. For Implement the cause-level repair · Debug K8s Network Layered Diagnosis, keep K8s Network Layered Diagnosis connected to mechanically reviewable Kubernetes object, status, and transfer evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: k8s-network-layered-diagnosis-12a5kw
  - competency: k8s-network-layered-diagnosis
    artifact: network-policy.yaml
    decision: network layered diagnosis
    environment: single-node minikube profile with a constrained Docker driver
    fault-injection: delete one ready Pod during a configuration rollout
    retained-proof: Pod state previous exit endpoint readiness and request result
    case-key: 12a5kw
    change: |
      pod-network: cni-implementation-boundary
      ip-family-policy: PreferDualStack
      ingress: default-deny-plus-gateway
      egress: default-deny-plus-dns-and-api
      metadata-endpoint: denied
      return-path: stateful-implementation-verified
    verify: kubectl get networkpolicy,pod,service,endpointslice -n intake -o json
    failure: Reject policy override semantics, automatic return-path claims, DNS-success as endpoint proof, or a CNI-independent packet conclusion.
    repair: Derive additive ingress and egress allows, preserve DNS and required control paths, then test name, endpoint, route, policy, and application layers.
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The structural check is only one gate. The following changed-case decision and explanation must also hold.

hints: For Implement the cause-level repair · Debug K8s Network Layered Diagnosis, name the context, namespace, API identity, desired state, observed status, controller, policy, and failure-domain boundary, K8s Network Layered Diagnosis precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Implement the cause-level repair · Debug K8s Network Layered Diagnosis, trace this concrete input and predict admission, defaulting, scheduling, reconciliation, status, traffic, storage, policy, disruption, and cleanup evidence before reviewing it: A debug Pod can resolve DNS but cannot reach a backend after default deny; the policy selects only ingress, misses egress DNS, and assumes NetworkPolicy implementation behavior. Trace source, destination, port, direction, selector, CNI, and node boundary. Competency probe: Diagnose name, endpoint, bind, policy, route, proxy, gateway, certificate, and application failures with source and destination evidence.

For Implement the cause-level repair · Debug K8s Network Layered Diagnosis, challenge the documented misconception with object, condition, event, policy, failure, repair, rollback, and transfer evidence: Successful DNS resolution proves the Service has reachable ready backends.
